channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 How California Politics Set the National Tone | Foreign Policy
item.description: Ronald Reagan honed his right-wing conservatism in the Golden State.

item.title: 📍 Regulatory Friendly Fire: How ITAR Undermines the Alliance It Was Built to Protect | War on the Rocks
item.description: For nearly a century, America’s defense industrial strength yielded a subtle benefit: influence as the world’s security guarantor of choice. But the system that once anchored partner access to U.S. weaponry is now an obstacle. Modern statecraft — the interplay of industry,…

item.title: 🌍 Army Aviation’s Wasted Decade: Lessons for the Next Generation of Drone Integration | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2006, the U.S. Army’s 25th Combat Aviation Brigade deployed to Iraq, where it paired Task Force ODIN (Observe, Detect, Identify, and Neutralize) with an Apache battalion from the 82nd Airborne Division — a first-of-its-kind teaming of attack helicopters with drones. These…

item.title: 🌍 Missiles Aren’t Strategy: Lessons From Iran for a Pacific Air War | War on the Rocks
item.description: Western airpower debates are increasingly driven by grim math. Analysts tally missile inventories, strike ranges, and sortie-generation capacity against a small number of critical runways, fuel systems, and aerial refueling aircraft. The prevailing conclusion is that U.S. and…

item.title: 📍 There Is a Solution to the Global Health Care Crisis | Foreign Policy
item.description: Cuts in foreign aid have been devastating. Countries have a window to step in and craft plans for success.

item.title: 🌏 In 2026, a lot of Taiwan’s military equipment is just deadweight | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Late in April, a batch of American-made M1A2 Abrams tanks arrived by sea in the Port of Taipei. The 28 M1A2s are the last of 108 Abrams tanks that Taiwan bought from the United States ...

item.title: 📍 A New Order for the Gulf | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The region must build its own security, not buy it.

item.title: 📍 Where Are The Carriers As Of May 17, 2026: Ford Is Finally Home | The War Zone
item.description: The Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group arrived home in Norfolk after a grueling, record-setting 326-day deployment. The post Where Are The Carriers As Of May 17, 2026: Ford Is Finally Home appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy E/A-18G Growlers Collide At Idaho Air Show | The War Zone
item.description: All four crew members ejected as the aircraft became entangled and plummeted to the ground. The post Navy E/A-18G Growlers Collide At Idaho Air Show appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Digitally watched without consent: spyware as a tool of coercive control | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Spyware, once primarily used by intelligence agencies and nation-states, has become a tool of intimate partner abuse, quietly deployed against people – mostly women – who have no idea they are being watched. In an ...

item.title: 📍 NDS 2026 – Focus on high-intensity conflict neglects peacekeeping and HADR | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australian defence policy focuses too much on the risk of high-intensity, mostly conventional conflict at large cost to capabilities for peacekeeping and humanitarian support, its most likely military operations. Many costly new equipment priorities, such ...

item.title: 🌏 Family Business: Dynastic Politics in the Philippines | The Diplomat
item.description: Pressure is building for a change.

item.title: 📍 Spheres by Default | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How U.S. concessions are quietly becoming Chinese influence.

item.title: 🌏 Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: Trump in Beijing: Respect, Leverage, and the New Realism of U.S.–China Relations | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: Trump in Beijing: Respect, Leverage, and the New Realism of U.S.–China Relations appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Digest of Recent Articles on Just Security (May 11-15, 2026) | Just Security
item.description: Symposium: Intersection of Sanctions and Corruption • Series: Syria in Transition • Series: When Guardrails Erode • Russia-Ukraine War • U.S. Military • Armed Conflict • International Human Rights • Transnational Repression • Global Governance and Democracy • AI Governance •…

item.title: 🏛️ Even as drones usher in an era of ‘cheap kill,’ Army leaders look to what’s next | Defense One
item.description: Uncrewed vehicles were everywhere at LANPAC—even above a general’s head.

item.title: 🌏 Mysterious Chinese SUV With Massive Roof Featured In Trump Motorcade In Beijing | The War Zone
item.description: The bizarre-looking high-top SUVs were among several notable vehicles spotted in President Trump's motorcade during his recent trip to China. The post Mysterious Chinese SUV With Massive Roof Featured In Trump Motorcade In Beijing appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army’s 7th Infantry Division, 1st MDTF to merge as Multi-Domain Command-Pacific | Defense News
item.description: The two-star Multi-Domain Command-Pacific will merge the 7th ID’s Stryker infantry brigades and a combat aviation brigade with a multidomain task force.

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: The water tastes metallic. The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Where’s the beef? Trump’s underwhelming meeting with Xi. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Trump appears to have sidestepped the biggest trap—Beijing’s attempt to box Washington in on Taiwan—but underperformed on everything else. The post Where’s the beef? Trump’s underwhelming meeting with Xi. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 What the Trump-Xi Summit Won't Solve | Foreign Affairs
item.description: A conversation with Orville Schell.

item.title: 📍 The problem with the US power-sharing plan for Libya | Atlantic Council
item.description: The nascent US initiative may succeed in reducing tensions and creating a slightly more predictable environment for foreign businesses, but a structural transformation remains out of reach. The post The problem with the US power-sharing plan for Libya appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🌍 Braw in Sky News on Iran threatening tariffs on undersea cables | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 15, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw was interviewed by Sky News on Iran threatening to place tariffs on vital undersea cables that support global connectivity. The post Braw in Sky News on Iran threatening tariffs on undersea cables appeared…

item.title: 📍 Don’t fall for the middle-power mirage | Atlantic Council
item.description: The kind of multilateralism that Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney spoke about in Davos remains an aspiration rather than an achievable reality. The post Don’t fall for the middle-power mirage appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 HASC leader threatens Pentagon with ‘pain’ over canceled Europe deployment | Defense One
item.description: Recent Trump-administration moves may drop troop presence below legal minimums.

item.title: 🕵️ CIA Director Ratcliffe’s Trip to Havana | Foreign Policy
item.description: The United States is offering to turn Cuba’s lights back on—for a price.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army to receive thousands of Barracuda-500M cruise missiles in Anduril deal | Defense News
item.description: The Pentagon’s Low-Cost Containerized Missiles program aims to obtain over 10,000 low-cost cruise missiles over a three-year span.

item.title: 📍 Argentina Retires Its A-4 Fightinghawks | The War Zone
item.description: After six decades of service, the A-4’s first export operator has finally stood down its Skyhawks as it introduces its first F-16s. The post Argentina Retires Its A-4 Fightinghawks appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of May 9: U.S. President Donald Trump visits China, India and Kenya host summits, and several prime ministers have a rough time.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth memo calls for sweeping, open-ended review of Pentagon's legal system | Defense One
item.description: It’s the secretary's latest unusual move toward DOD’s military and civilian lawyers.
